questions.question: Explain the Node.js event loop.
questions.explanation: Single-threaded but non-blocking. Event loop handles callbacks. Phases: timers, pending callbacks, idle/prepare, poll (I/O), check, close. Microtasks (promises) run between phases. libuv library manages the loop.

questions.question: What are streams in Node.js?
questions.explanation: Streams process data in chunks rather than loading entirely. Types: readable, writable, duplex, transform. Benefits: memory efficient, composable. Examples: file reading, HTTP requests/responses, compression.

questions.question: What is middleware in Express?
questions.explanation: Middleware functions process requests/responses. Called in order. Can modify req/res, end request, or pass to next middleware. Signature: (req, res, next) => {}. Used for authentication, logging, error handling.

questions.question: What is the difference between process.nextTick and setImmediate?
questions.explanation: nextTick: microtask, executed after current operation completes. setImmediate: macrotask, executed in next event loop iteration. nextTick higher priority. Generally use setImmediate for heavy work.
